final_prompt.prompt:
Create an editorial photo of a famous Zen temple's karesansui dry landscape garden in Kyoto, Japan.

Style/medium: architectural photography, photorealistic, wide-angle lens, inspired by Japanese minimalist aesthetic
Composition: wide-angle panoramic view, low-angle shot emphasizing geometric patterns and spatial depth, foreground-to-background extension
Subject: meticulously raked white gravel forming concentric circles and ripple patterns symbolizing ocean waves, moss-covered rocks of varied shapes representing islands and mountain peaks
Scene/backdrop: traditional Zen temple garden in Kyoto, low wooden corridor with evenly spaced pillars casting orderly shadows, ancient earthen wall with moss growing along the top
Lighting/mood: late afternoon autumn sunlight, warm oblique rays casting elongated shadows, serene and meditative atmosphere with profound Eastern aesthetic
Color palette: warm off-white gravel as base, muted moss greens and earthy browns, subdued and restrained tones
Materials/textures: fine white gravel with precise rake marks, velvety moss on weathered rocks, aged wood grain, rough earthen wall surface
Avoid: oversaturated colors, modern elements, people, artificial lighting, 8K stuffer aesthetic, deformed rocks
